visual_features.overall_style: Corporate and service-oriented with a moderately modern look. Flat UI with strong photographic hero imagery, clean sans-serif typography, and a bright aqua + orange brand palette. Visual emphasis on professionalism (office scene) and product clarity (water bottle foreground).
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Primary hierarchy driven by color contrast and scale: (1) Top navigation bar with large logo on the left and a high-contrast orange 'ORDER' button on the right; (2) Full-width hero photograph with the water bottle and glass in the front and a person at a laptop in the background; (3) Order/contact form band directly under the hero, laid out in three columns, with form fields on the left and center and service information text on the right. Within the form, the leftmost dropdown and name fields appear first, followed by email/phone, then text info block.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate to generous whitespace. The header has compact vertical padding but substantial horizontal spacing between navigation items. The hero image occupies a large uninterrupted area, providing visual breathing room. The order form section uses ample padding around the fields, with clear gaps between columns and sufficient vertical spacing between each input, creating clarity without feeling empty.
unique_elements: Full-width hero image featuring a prominently placed branded water bottle and glass on a desk, with a large bouquet of red roses to the left and a blurred person working at a laptop in the background.
Top-right floating circular social media icons and a circular national flag that sit over the hero image rather than inside the navigation bar.
Bright orange right-aligned 'ORDER' navigation item styled as a CTA button that visually separates itself from the standard nav links.
Three-column order/contact band immediately below the hero where the left and middle columns are form fields and the right column is a text-based service schedule and phone contact block.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid, with the order/contact section indicating three equal-width columns (approximately 4 columns each) and full-width sections for header and hero.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width top navigation bar, followed by a full-bleed hero image, then a full-width multi-column content band (order/contact form). General top-to-bottom scrolling layout.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: 1) Sticky-looking horizontal header with logo left, central navigation links, and CTA button right; 2) Hero section with centered photographic content spanning the full viewport width; 3) Directly beneath, a white-background band that contains the customer type dropdown, name, email, and phone inputs, plus service hours and phone number text. Subsequent sections (not visible) likely continue the pattern of horizontally full-width content separated by color bands or whitespace.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header elements aligned horizontally using a central axis: logo left, navigation in the middle, CTA right. Navigation labels are horizontally centered within their space. Form fields are left-aligned within each column. Text block on the right column is left-aligned and set in paragraphs/lines. Overall alignment follows a strong left baseline within each content column, with consistent vertical alignment between adjacent form fields.
responsive_hints: Three-column form/order band suggests stacking behavior on smaller screens (3 columns likely collapse to 1 or 2 columns).
Full-width hero image indicates background-size: cover behavior, with focal point centered on the bottle and laptop.
Navigation bar with many items suggests a breakpoint at which links might collapse into a hamburger or stacked menu to avoid crowding.
Circular social and flag icons placed near the right edge imply they may reposition below the hero or into a footer/social bar on mobile.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: (1) A full-width header bar with logo, horizontal navigation items, and a right-aligned CTA button; (2) A full-bleed hero image that spans the width of the page, with focal content roughly centered horizontally; (3) Directly beneath, a white band functioning as an order/contact area, divided into three vertical columns: left column with customer-type dropdown and name input stacked vertically, middle column with email and phone inputs stacked vertically, right column with stacked text lines for customer service hours and a call-to-action phone number.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header occupies a shallow horizontal strip (~10–15% of the initial viewport height). The hero image dominates the first screen (~45–55% of viewport height) and is visually prominent. The order/contact band beneath uses the remaining space on the initial fold (~25–35%), with roughly equal column widths, each separated by generous horizontal gutters. Within each column, inputs and text blocks are given clear vertical spacing, with approximately 16–24px between fields, avoiding crowding.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate density leaning toward spacious. Large hero imagery creates breathing room. The navigation bar has multiple items but ample horizontal space, preventing a cramped feel. The order/contact form has several fields but is distributed across three columns, making each column feel manageable. Inputs have sizeable padding and room around them, contributing to a clean, easy-to-scan interface rather than an information-dense dashboard.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The user’s eye starts at the top-left logo, moves horizontally across the primary navigation to the high-contrast orange 'ORDER' button on the right (Z-pattern along the top). From there, the eye drops down to the center of the hero image where the water bottle and glass are positioned. The line of sight then extends rightward toward the laptop and blurred person, reinforcing a professional office context. As the eye continues downward, it lands on the order/contact form band, typically starting at the left column (customer dropdown and name) and naturally progressing across to the email/phone fields and finally to the service hours and telephone information on the right.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary CTA (ORDER) is a rounded rectangle with approximately 4–6px corner radius. It uses a solid orange fill (#FFC145), white text in a medium or semibold sans-serif, and no visible border. The height appears around 36–44px with horizontal padding of ~16–24px, making it clearly more prominent than standard navigation items. Hover state is likely a slightly darker orange and maybe a subtle drop shadow or underline for affordance. Social icons are circular 'icon buttons' with a solid brand color background (e.g., Facebook blue) and white icon centered within a perfect circle, likely ~36–40px diameter.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Primary CTA likely implemented as an anchor tag styled as a button (e.g., ORDER) inside the navigation bar. Social buttons likely use with nested or SVG icon elements, wrapped in a circular style via border-radius: 50%.

ui_patterns.visual_description: There are no explicit standalone cards visible, but the order/contact band behaves like a large full-width card: white background, subtle separation from the hero through a horizontal rule or color change, with content divided into columns. Each column reads as a soft card-like segment: flat design (no noticeable shadows), thin light-grey borders around inputs, and generous internal padding. Edges appear to have sharp or minimally rounded corners (0–2px).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical card structure would be a <div class="card"> container with padding, containing a heading or label, followed by content (form controls or text). For potential product/service cards elsewhere on the site, structure would likely follow: image or icon at top, title, then descriptive text and a CTA link or button, all vertically stacked inside the container.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form inputs are full-width within their column, rectangular with sharp corners or a very small radius (~2px). They have a 1px light-grey border (#E0E0E0–#E5E5E5), white background, and left-aligned placeholder text in a lighter grey. Vertical spacing between fields appears to be ~16–24px. Labels in this visible snippet are primarily using placeholder text inside the fields rather than external labels. Dropdowns follow the same style but with a right-aligned caret icon indicating selection. The right column contains plain text, not form controls. Density is moderate, with enough spacing to avoid a clinical form feeling.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms are likely structured as a standard <form> containing stacked <div class="form-group"> wrappers per field. Within each group, a <label> (possibly visually hidden) precedes an <input> or <select>. Columns are arranged using a grid system (e.g., Bootstrap or CSS flexbox), such as <div class="row"> containing three <div class="col-md-4"> elements for the left, middle, and right segments. Validation styles (not shown) likely rely on border color changes and inline helper text under fields.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Navigation is a horizontal bar aligned at the top of the page, with a solid aqua background (#45B4D7). The logo sits on the left inside a white rectangular block (top-left) featuring a blue logomark and the wordmark 'NAVARO' plus a tagline. Navigation items ('Company Profile', 'Product', 'Contact', etc.) are inline links with no visible dividers, spaced with generous horizontal padding. Text is likely medium-weight sans-serif in white. The 'ORDER' item is visually differentiated as a solid orange button. Active or hover states for standard links are likely indicated via text color changes (to a darker shade) or a subtle underline, while the ORDER button likely darkens slightly on hover.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Underlying structure is most likely a semantic <nav> element containing an unordered list (<ul>) of list items (<li>) each wrapping an <a> link. The logo is housed in a separate container aligned left, and the navigation list is center-aligned or flex-distributed across the remaining horizontal space. The ORDER button is probably one of the <li> items with an additional class (e.g., .nav-cta) applying button styling.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The service hours content on the right side of the order/contact section is presented as a vertical text list without bullet markers, each line on a separate row. Line spacing is moderate (roughly 1.4–1.6), ensuring readability. No visible separators or icons between list lines; instead, hierarchy is conveyed via line breaks and possibly subtle typographic contrast (e.g., bolder 'Customer service and Delivery' label followed by regular-weight schedule text). Navigation menu items also form a horizontal list with equal spacing and no separators.

heading.visual_style: Headings and navigation text appear to use a clean, neutral sans-serif with relatively uniform stroke widths and a straightforward, corporate feel. It resembles a geometric/humanist sans with clear letterforms and no decorative features.

body.visual_style: Body text looks like a standard, readable sans-serif (similar to Arial/Helvetica), optimized for legibility with medium x-height and neutral character shapes.

spacing_system.component_padding: Forms and buttons appear to use internal padding based on 8px multiples (e.g., inputs at 8–12px vertical and 12–16px horizontal; buttons around 8–10px vertical and 16–20px horizontal). Navigation items have roughly 16–24px horizontal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header-to-hero, hero-to-form) appears to be 32–48px visually, often achieved via padding on the sections rather than explicit margins.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change text color or underline on hover, while the ORDER button likely darkens its background or adds a subtle shadow to indicate interactivity.
Form inputs are expected to highlight on focus with a darker or colored border (e.g., primary blue) and possibly a subtle inner shadow or glow.
Social media circular buttons probably scale up slightly or shift to a lighter/darker shade of their brand color on hover, with a cursor change to pointer.
Transitions between color states (hover/focus) are likely implemented using short (150–250ms) ease-in-out CSS transitions for background-color, border-color, and box-shadow.

Micro-interactions for form validation (not visible) could involve red border and small error text under the field, with shake or subtle fade-in animations; success states might use a green border or icon.
